title: Tell a layered story
subtitle: Event, emotion, meaning.
overview: Module: Extended Speaking. B2 Med strengthens abstract discussion, source response, professional communication, precise style, revision, and independent output.

sections.title: B2 med focus
rows: A layered story includes what happened, how it felt, and why it matters.
有层次的故事包括发生了什么、感觉如何，以及为什么重要。
B2 Med frame: open clearly, develop two layers, handle a complication, and close with a takeaway.
B2 Med 框架：清楚开场、展开两层、处理复杂点，并用收获结尾。
Tell a layered story about learning.
讲一个关于学习的有层次故事。

The event was simple: I joined a discussion and made mistakes. Emotionally, I felt exposed. But the meaning was important because I learned that communication can continue even with imperfect language.
事件很简单：我参加了一场讨论并犯了错。情感上，我觉得自己暴露在外。但意义很重要，因为我学到即使语言不完美，交流也可以继续。

items.en: A layered story includes what happened, how it felt, and why it matters.
items.zh: 有层次的故事包括发生了什么、感觉如何，以及为什么重要。

items.en: B2 Med frame: open clearly, develop two layers, handle a complication, and close with a takeaway.
items.zh: B2 Med 框架：清楚开场、展开两层、处理复杂点，并用收获结尾。

items.en: The event was simple: I joined a discussion and made mistakes. Emotionally, I felt exposed. But the meaning was important because I learned that communication can continue even with imperfect language.
items.zh: 事件很简单：我参加了一场讨论并犯了错。情感上，我觉得自己暴露在外。但意义很重要，因为我学到即使语言不完美，交流也可以继续。

items.en: I will make the idea precise before I try to make it sound advanced.
items.zh: 我会先让意思准确，再尝试让它听起来高级。

items.en: A strong B2 answer usually includes a condition, a limitation, or a fair counterpoint.
items.zh: 强 B2 回答通常包括条件、限制或公平的反方观点。

items.en: If the wording feels too abstract, I will add one concrete example from real life.
items.zh: 如果措辞感觉太抽象，我会加入一个来自真实生活的具体例子。

items.en: I can revise my answer for logic, tone, and rhythm.
items.zh: 我可以从逻辑、语气和节奏上修改我的回答。

items.en: This lesson is complete only when I can use the idea in my own words.
items.zh: 只有当我能用自己的话使用这个想法时，这节课才算完成。

lines.speaker: A
lines.en: Tell a layered story about learning.
lines.zh: 讲一个关于学习的有层次故事。

lines.speaker: B
lines.en: The event was simple: I joined a discussion and made mistakes. Emotionally, I felt exposed. But the meaning was important because I learned that communication can continue even with imperfect language.
lines.zh: 事件很简单：我参加了一场讨论并犯了错。情感上，我觉得自己暴露在外。但意义很重要，因为我学到即使语言不完美，交流也可以继续。

lines.speaker: A
lines.en: Can you make the answer more precise or more concrete?
lines.zh: 你能让回答更准确或更具体吗？

lines.speaker: B
lines.en: Yes. I will narrow the claim, add one example, and mention one limitation.
lines.zh: 可以。我会缩小主张、加入一个例子，并提到一个限制。

items.en: Tell a layered story about learning.
items.zh: 讲一个关于学习的有层次故事。

items.en: Answer once in six sentences with a clear structure.
items.zh: 用六句话做一次结构清楚的回答。

items.en: Add one B2 phrase such as that said, in practice, arguably, or the real issue is.
items.zh: 加入一个 B2 短语，比如 that said、in practice、arguably 或 the real issue is。

items.en: Record the answer, then rewrite the strongest sentence more naturally.
items.zh: 录下回答，然后把最强的一句改得更自然。

items.en: Use the revised sentence tomorrow in a different topic.
items.zh: 明天在不同话题中使用修改后的句子。
